name: Fried Shrimp Rolls (Cha Gio Tom)
recipeIngredient: 4 oz lean ground beef, finely chopped
4 oz ground pork shoulder, finely chopped
4 oz fresh or canned crab meat, picked over and drained
Rice paper: 24 rounds, each 8 1/2 inches in diameter
Raw medium shrimp, peeled and deveined, with tails attached
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Nuoc Cham by mixing equal parts fish sauce and water.
2. Wash and dry mint and coriander leaves; set aside.
3. Soak cellophane noodles in warm water for 30 minutes, then drain.
4. Assemble the rolls:
a. Dissolve sugar in 4 cups of warm water.
b. Keep remaining rice paper sheets covered with a damp cloth to prevent curling.
c. Immerse one sheet at a time into the warm water until soft.
d. Place a shrimp in the center and add filling.
e. Fold sides over, then fold up the bottom third.
f. Form a compact triangle shape with the tail extending over the fold line.
g. Press to seal; repeat for remaining rolls.
5. Fry the rolls:
a. Heat oil in a skillet to 325°F (163°C).
b. Add a few rolls at a time and fry until crisp and golden brown, about 10-12 minutes, turning often.
c. Drain on paper towels; keep warm while frying remaining rolls.
6. Serve the shrimp rolls with Nuoc Cham, mint, and coriander leaves.
Yield: 24 shrimp rolls.
